questions.text: What is the slope of the line passing through points (1, 2) and (4, 8)?
The equation of a line with slope 2 that passes through (0, 3) is:
questions.explanation: Using y = mx + c with slope 2 and intercept 3 gives y = 2x + 3.

questions.text: The slope of a line parallel to 3x - 4y + 5 = 0 is:
questions.explanation: The line has slope 3/4, so any parallel line has the same slope.

questions.text: The line perpendicular to y = 2x + 1 has slope:
questions.explanation: Perpendicular slopes are negative reciprocals, so slope = -1/2.

questions.text: The x-intercept of the line y = 2x + 4 is:
questions.explanation: Set y = 0, then 0 = 2x + 4 gives x = -2.

questions.text: The distance from point (3, 4) to the line x + y - 7 = 0 is:
questions.explanation: The point satisfies the line equation, so the distance is 0.

questions.text: The angle θ between two lines with slopes m1 and m2 is given by tan θ = :
questions.explanation: This formula calculates the acute angle between two intersecting lines.

questions.text: If three points A, B, and C are collinear, then:
options: Slope of AB = Slope of BC
Slope of AB × Slope of BC = -1
Slope of AB + Slope of BC = 0
questions.explanation: Collinear points lie on the same line, so the slopes of segments between them must be equal.

questions.text: The equation of a line in intercept form is:
options: x cos α + y sin α = p
questions.explanation: Here 'a' and 'b' are the x and y intercepts respectively.

questions.text: The equation of the x-axis is:
questions.explanation: Every point on the x-axis has a y-coordinate of 0.

questions.text: The equation of a line passing through (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) is:
questions.explanation: This is the two-point form of the equation of a line.

questions.text: What is the slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has θ = 0°, so tan 0° = 0.

questions.text: What is the slope of a vertical line?
questions.explanation: A vertical line has θ = 90°, and tan 90° is undefined.

questions.text: Find the equation of a line which makes intercepts -3 and 2 on the x and y axes respectively.
The distance between parallel lines Ax + By + C1 = 0 and Ax + By + C2 = 0 is:
questions.explanation: This formula calculates the perpendicular distance between two parallel lines.

questions.text: The slope of the line x + √3y + 1 = 0 is:
Equation of a line passing through origin with slope m is:
questions.explanation: At the origin, the intercept c = 0.

questions.text: Find the distance of the point (3, -5) from the line 3x - 4y - 26 = 0.
If the line y = mx + c passes through (2, 4) and (3, 6), find m and c.
The normal form of a line is x cos ω + y sin ω = p. Here 'p' is:
questions.explanation: In normal form, p is the length of the perpendicular from the origin to the line.

questions.text: The condition for two lines a1x + b1y + c1 = 0 and a2x + b2y + c2 = 0 to be parallel is:
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have equal slopes: -a1/b1 = -a2/b2.

questions.text: The condition for two lines a1x + b1y + c1 = 0 and a2x + b2y + c2 = 0 to be perpendicular is:
Find the slope of a line which makes an angle of 150° with the positive direction of x-axis.
Reduce x + √3y - 4 = 0 to normal form. The value of p is:
Which of the following lines passes through the origin?
questions.explanation: A line passes through the origin if it has no constant term (c=0).

questions.text: The point of intersection of the lines x - y = 0 and x + y = 2 is:
questions.explanation: Adding equations gives 2x = 2 => x=1. Then y=1.

questions.text: The lines 2x + 3y - 7 = 0 and 3x - 2y + 4 = 0 are:
What is the equation of a line parallel to the y-axis and 3 units to the right?
questions.explanation: Lines parallel to the y-axis have the form x = k.

questions.text: Find the distance between the parallel lines 3x - 4y + 7 = 0 and 3x - 4y + 5 = 0.
The area of a triangle with vertices (x1, y1), (x2, y2), (x3, y3) is zero if the points are:
questions.explanation: Area is zero if and only if the points lie on a straight line.

questions.text: The coordinates of the foot of the perpendicular from (0,0) to x + y - 2 = 0 are:
questions.explanation: The line through origin perpendicular to x+y-2=0 is y=x. Intersection is (1,1).

questions.text: The inclination of the line x - y + 3 = 0 is:
Find the equation of a line passing through (2, 2) such that the sum of its intercepts on the axes is 9.
questions.explanation: x/a + y/(9-a) = 1. Passes through (2,2) gives 2/a + 2/(9-a) = 1. a=3 or a=6.

questions.text: What is the slope of the line perpendicular to the line joining (2, 3) and (4, 1)?
questions.explanation: Slope of line = (1-3)/(4-2) = -1. Perpendicular slope = 1.

questions.text: The shifting of origin to a new point (h, k) without changing direction of axes is called:
questions.explanation: This transformation is known as translation of axes.

questions.text: If the origin is shifted to (1, 1), the new coordinates of point (4, 4) will be:
Equation of a line parallel to x-axis and passing through (a, b) is:
questions.explanation: A line parallel to the x-axis has a constant y-coordinate.

questions.text: If the angle between two lines is π/4 and slope of one line is 1/2, find the slope of other line.
The lines y = m1x + c1 and y = m2x + c2 are coincident if:
questions.explanation: Coincident lines are identical, so both slope and intercept must match.

questions.text: The general equation of a line Ax + By + C = 0 represents a straight line if:
options: A and B are both zero
At least one of A or B is non-zero
questions.explanation: If both A and B are zero, the equation no longer represents a line in the plane.
